antipatterns.name: Flat hierarchy
antipatterns.bad: All text is 14px / font-weight: 400 / same color
antipatterns.why: Users cannot scan — every element competes equally
antipatterns.fix: Apply at least 3 distinct size/weight/color levels per screen

antipatterns.name: ALL-CAPS body text
antipatterns.why: ALL CAPS removes word shape silhouettes — reading speed drops ~12%
antipatterns.fix: Reserve uppercase for overlines (11px, tracking-widest) only

antipatterns.name: Bold on small text
antipatterns.why: Bold at 11–12px causes ink trapping — counters (a, e, o) fill in visually
antipatterns.fix: Use font-medium (500) at small sizes, reserve bold for 16px+

antipatterns.name: Low-contrast 'design choice'
antipatterns.bad: text-gray-300 on white background (#D1D5DB = 1.6:1 — fails WCAG)
antipatterns.why: 10% of users have some form of visual impairment
antipatterns.fix: text-secondary (#52525B) minimum for any meaningful text

antipatterns.name: Arbitrary size values
antipatterns.why: Breaks the scale — 6 developers each pick different 'between' sizes
antipatterns.fix: Add a missing token to the scale config instead

antipatterns.name: Letter-spacing on body
antipatterns.why: Increases visual noise, reduces reading speed — font designer already set spacing
antipatterns.fix: tracking-normal on all body and mixed-case UI text. Always.

antipatterns.name: Justified text
antipatterns.why: CSS justification creates irregular word spacing — 'rivers' of whitespace

antipatterns.name: Center-aligned paragraphs
antipatterns.bad: This is a long description that spans multiple lines...
antipatterns.why: Ragged left edge makes each line's start position unpredictable — hard to read
antipatterns.fix: text-center only for 1–2 line standalone copy (hero taglines, empty states)

scale: All font sizes use defined tokens — no arbitrary text-[Npx] values
Adjacent heading levels are visually distinct when placed side by side
text-display is used only in marketing/hero contexts
hierarchy: Every screen has at least 3 distinct visual levels (size, weight, or color)
Primary action / most important content is visually dominant
There is no element competing for 'most important' with the actual primary element
weight: No font-bold on body copy or descriptions
At most 2 weights used within any single component
color: No text-tertiary on interactive or meaningful text
Semantic colors (danger, success) always paired with an icon
All text passes WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 normal, 3:1 large)
spacing: More space above headings than below (heading belongs to content that follows)
No letter-spacing on mixed-case body or UI text
accessibility: No color as the only differentiator for interactive text (underline or border also present)
No opacity-40 on text that carries meaning without aria-disabled or equivalent
Font size minimum 16px for body, 14px absolute floor for any text
